data.description:
## Why
Workflows need to call **external MCP servers** (serena, github, Atlassian, …) as a
first-class, deterministic step kind — the capability rebar's scripted ops cannot provide
(rebar's own ticket ops stay as scripted ops; this is for third-party tools). This is the
"deterministic MCP step" pattern (Dify/Rivet): the workflow author picks the call, not an LLM.
It is carved out of the foundation epic because it is a separable subsystem with its own
security surface (allowlist/secrets/manifest), is env-gated, and depends on the contract model
the foundation establishes while nothing in the foundation depends on it.

## Depends on
The foundation epic (contract-bearing prompt/step model): this epic surfaces an MCP tool's
`inputSchema`/`outputSchema` through the SAME contract/inspector machinery the foundation builds.
Build only after the foundation's contract registry, validation, and editor inspector land.

## What (settled design — see foundation epic decision-log)
- **`mcp_call` leaf step kind.** Same shape as a scripted step (with-in, named-outputs-out);
  add to the v2 IR schema `oneOf`, the step-kind classifier, and executor dispatch. Reuse
  pydantic_ai's MCP client (on the official `mcp` SDK, already an optional dep) for stdio + http;
  drop to the SDK's `ClientSession` for manifest work. NO hand-rolled transport/handshake.
- **Two-tier allowlist + trust.** rebar SHIPS a default allowlist; projects ADD servers via
  their own `.rebar/` config; secrets live in PROJECT config via `${{ secrets.x }}` indirection
  (never inline). Resolution mirrors prompts (built-in ⊕ project) BUT projects may only ADD —
  they CANNOT shadow/redefine a built-in server name. The allowlist pins endpoint IDENTITY
  (not just name) and IS the trust boundary (Flowise RCE CVE-2025-59528 lesson). A workflow may
  call only an allow-listed `server.tool`; else lint/runtime refusal. Env-gated by a new
  `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_MCP_CALL`-style flag, default off. MCP tool annotations
  (readOnlyHint/destructiveHint/idempotentHint/openWorldHint) refine UX/defaults but are
  UNTRUSTED hints (default worst-case when absent); the allowlist enforces.
- **Cached manifest + explicit refresh.** Introspect a server once (`tools/list`, following
  `nextCursor` pagination), cache tool list + `inputSchema`s to `.rebar/mcp-tools/<server>.json`;
  the editor palette reads the CACHE (offline-capable, git-reviewable, deterministic). Explicit
  `rebar mcp refresh <server>` re-introspects; invalidate on `notifications/tools/list_changed`;
  stamp the manifest with a version/TTL. Run-time re-validates args against the live `inputSchema`
  and reconciles the server endpoint against the trusted allowlist.
- **Output handling.** When a tool declares `outputSchema`, validate `structuredContent` against
  it and feed those fields to the expression validator. When absent (the common case — and
  observed even when outputSchema IS declared, per spike E3), treat the result `content[]` as
  opaque `any`; handle `isError: true`. Opaque `any` is NOT silently mappable into a typed
  downstream input contract without an author-acknowledged cast.
- **Auth.** stdio = env credentials; http = OAuth 2.1 — modeled SEPARATELY; lean on the client
  lib, don't store bare tokens.

## Relevant code
- IR + dispatch: `src/rebar/schemas/workflow.v2.schema.json`,
  `src/rebar/llm/workflow/{schema,executor,lint_refs}.py`.
- MCP client: pydantic_ai (`[agents]`) + `mcp` SDK (`[mcp]`), both already present.
- Allowlist/secrets config: `.rebar/` (mirrors the prompt two-tier resolution).

## Context
Designed in the foundation epic's /joe-brainstorm and derisked by spike E3 (introspect → manifest
→ call against the real serena server via the already-present SDK: paginated `tools/list`, all
tools advertise `inputSchema`, both structuredContent and opaque `content[]` handled, `isError`
graceful, bad args don't crash the client). Zero new runtime dependencies.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `mcp_call` is a first-class v2 IR step kind (schema + classifier + executor dispatch) that
      calls an external MCP server's tool with `with:` inputs and produces named outputs.
- [ ] Two-tier allowlist (built-in ⊕ project-add-only, no shadowing) with endpoint-identity
      pinning; secrets via `${{ secrets.x }}`; calls outside the allowlist refused; env-gated.
- [ ] Cached tool manifest with `nextCursor` pagination, `list_changed` invalidation, explicit
      refresh; editor palette renders each tool's `inputSchema` as typed fields from the cache.
- [ ] Output handling: validate `structuredContent` vs `outputSchema` when present; opaque-`any`
      fallback otherwise (incl. when outputSchema is declared but absent in results); `isError` handled.
- [ ] stdio (env) and http (OAuth 2.1) auth modeled separately; no bare-token storage.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] A workflow author can add an allow-listed external MCP tool call as a deterministic step,
      see its inputs typed in the palette, wire its (possibly opaque) output downstream with the
      validator behaving correctly, and run it under the env gate — with the trust boundary enforced.
data.title: Workflow authoring v2: external MCP-call step kind (allowlist, secrets, manifest)
